symptoms.symptom_name: Sudden massive rigid abdominal distension after a rapid binge

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The mechanism (rapid large binge) plus rapid massive distension is highly specific for acute gastric dilatation in PWS.
Generalised epigastric tympanic distension is not the pattern of localised appendiceal peritonitis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Repeated vomiting of undigested, non-bilious food

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Undigested-food, non-bilious vomiting indicates retained gastric contents proximal to the outlet — consistent with a grossly distended/obstructed stomach.
Non-bilious undigested vomiting localises the problem at or above the gastric outlet.

symptoms.symptom_name: Tense, tympanic distension with global tenderness and voluntary guarding

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute massive gastric dilatation with impending necrosis/perforation
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Clinician-only sign of a surgical abdomen — sealed; the AI infers severity from the reported hard distended painful belly, not from palpation.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute massive gastric dilatation with impending necrosis/perforation
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Ileus/peritoneal irritation; auscultatory finding unavailable in telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Tachycardia (HR 125), pale and diaphoretic — early hypovolaemia

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute massive gastric dilatation with impending necrosis/perforation
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Third-spacing and pain drive tachycardia; measured HR/BP are sealed clinician data — the AI infers circulatory stress from the reported pale, sweaty, pounding-heart picture.

symptoms.symptom_name: Rapid shallow breathing / cannot lie flat (diaphragmatic splinting)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute massive gastric dilatation with impending necrosis/perforation
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Massive gastric distension splints the diaphragm and raises aspiration risk.

symptoms.symptom_name: Known Prader-Willi syndrome with hyperphagia (risk factor)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: PWS hyperphagia is the substrate for binge-related acute gastric dilatation and confers the specific rupture/necrosis risk.
Absence of diarrhoea and prodrome argues against gastroenteritis as the cause of the distension.
Non-bilious vomiting points to a proximal (gastric) rather than distal obstruction.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Acute massive gastric dilatation with impending necrosis/perforation
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Together, a rapid binge in PWS + massive rigid tympanic distension + undigested-food vomiting + absent bowel sounds is a surgical emergency until proven otherwise.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: A rigid distended tender abdomen with systemic upset needs hands-on surgical evaluation now; this is the telehealth-appropriate 'investigation request' — escalate, do not attempt remotely.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Erect/supine abdominal X-ray and/or CT abdomen
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: To confirm massive gastric dilatation and detect free intraperitoneal air (perforation) / gastric wall compromise — hospital only.
Both diagnostic and life-saving therapeutic decompression of the distended stomach — performed in the ED, not via telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Oral analgesia trial at home before assessment
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Contraindicated — the child must be nil by mouth; any oral intake worsens distension and aspiration risk and delays surgical care.
